source_text: An implementation may receive an unencrypted record of type change_cipher_spec consisting of the single byte value 0x01 at any time after the first ClientHello message has been sent or received and before the peer's Finished message has been received and MUST simply drop it without further processing.
rule_statement: Implementations MUST silently drop unencrypted CCS records (0x01) received between first ClientHello and peer's Finished.
applies_when: Receiving unencrypted CCS (0x01) between ClientHello and Finished
checkability_rationale: Can send CCS during handshake and verify it's ignored.

source_text: An implementation which receives any other change_cipher_spec value or which receives a protected change_cipher_spec record MUST abort the handshake with an "unexpected_message" alert.
rule_statement: Implementations receiving CCS with value other than 0x01 or a protected CCS MUST abort with unexpected_message.
applies_when: Receiving CCS with invalid value or protected CCS
checkability_rationale: Can send CCS with invalid value and verify alert.

source_text: If an implementation detects a change_cipher_spec record received before the first ClientHello message or after the peer's Finished message, it MUST be treated as an unexpected record type.
rule_statement: CCS received before first ClientHello or after peer's Finished MUST be treated as unexpected record type.
applies_when: Receiving CCS outside the valid window (before CH or after Finished)
checkability_rationale: Can send CCS at wrong time and verify rejection.

source_text: Implementations MUST NOT send record types not defined in this document unless negotiated by some extension.
rule_statement: Implementations MUST NOT send record types not defined in TLS 1.3 unless negotiated by extension.
checkability_rationale: Can send undefined record type and verify peer sends unexpected_message.

source_text: If a TLS implementation receives an unexpected record type, it MUST terminate the connection with an "unexpected_message" alert.
rule_statement: Receiving an unexpected record type MUST result in termination with unexpected_message alert.
applies_when: Receiving a record with undefined or unexpected ContentType
checkability_rationale: Can send record with unknown type and verify alert.
